schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 8f449a101b2b55d8a5f9f1a160ca4364fb1ccf7a
findings:
  - { id: judged-dc3-plumbing-leak-vs-wall-receipts-dc3, kind: judged, text: "badge-computes dc-3 routes findings by a two-way test (anchored → card, unanchored-but-path-in-spec-directory → case file) and names only two drop categories (repo-wide loci, decode failures), but wall-receipts dc-3 requires a third category: store-structural/plumbing findings (status-path, dangling layout keys, data-tracking) stay in verdi lint/CI entirely off the wall. A plumbing finding whose Path falls inside the spec's directory (e.g. a dangling layout key in the spec's own layout file) is unanchored and spec-local, so the story's mechanism surfaces it as a case-file badge — contradicting the parent's off-the-wall ruling. The story claims 'exactly wall-receipts dc-3' but its mechanism does not reproduce the parent's plumbing exclusion, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared. (confidence 0.45)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "the finding was correct against the first draft and the spec was amended in response, not argued past: dc-3 no longer routes by unanchored-but-path-in-spec-directory — the lint seam now carries a declared wall locus (object anchor or spec-level marker) and a finding that declares no locus stays off the wall fail-closed, even when its path lies inside the spec's own directory, reproducing wall-receipts dc-3's third (plumbing) bucket exactly; with the amended mechanism the contradiction no longer exists", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }

A plumbing finding whose Path falls inside the spec's directory (e.g. a dangling layout key in the spec's own layout file) is unanchored and spec-local, so the story's mechanism surfaces it as a case-file badge — contradicting the parent's off-the-wall ruling. # Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dc3-plumbing-leak-vs-wall-receipts-dc3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: badge-computes dc-3 routes findings by a two-way test (anchored → card, unanchored-but-path-in-spec-directory → case file) and names only two drop categories (repo-wide loci, decode failures), but wall-receipts dc-3 requires a third category: store-structural/plumbing findings (status-path, dangling layout keys, data-tracking) stay in verdi lint/CI entirely off the wall. A plumbing finding whose Path falls inside the spec's directory (e.g. a dangling layout key in the spec's own layout file) is unanchored and spec-local, so the story's mechanism surfaces it as a case-file badge — contradicting the parent's off-the-wall ruling. The story claims 'exactly wall-receipts dc-3' but its mechanism does not reproduce the parent's plumbing exclusion, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared. (confidence 0.45)
